# Mohamed Alhaskir

> Mohamed Alhaskir is a Syrian-German machine learning researcher and engineer, and a PhD candidate at the Institute of Medical Informatics, RWTH Aachen University (Aachen, Germany). His work focuses on trustworthy and multimodal AI for medicine — building reliable, reproducible, and deployable ML systems for high-stakes biomedical settings.

## About

- **Name:** Mohamed Alhaskir
- **Role:** PhD Candidate in Medical AI · Machine Learning Researcher & Engineer
- **Affiliation:** Institute of Medical Informatics, RWTH Aachen University, Aachen, Germany
- **Nationality:** Syrian-German
- **Website:** https://mohamed-alhaskir.github.io/
- **Email:** malhaskir@ukaachen.de

## Research focus

- Trustworthy AI (robustness, reproducibility, auditability, evaluation in high-stakes settings)
- Medical AI and biomedical machine learning
- Multimodal learning and large language models
- Dependable, deployable ML systems and reproducible workflows
- Wearable ECG-based seizure detection and clinical AI evaluation

## Selected publications

- Reliable Detection of Focal Onset Impaired Awareness Seizures Using Wearable ECG (Alhaskir, M., et al., Computer Methods and Programs in Biomedicine, 2026)
- ECG Matching: Synchronizing ECG Datasets for Data Quality Comparisons (Alhaskir, M., et al., Studies in Health Technology and Informatics, 2023)
- The Role of Self-Supervised Pretraining in Differentially Private Medical Image Analysis (co-author, arXiv preprint, 2026)

## Open-source projects

- MoniPy — open-source ML pipeline for wearable ECG-based seizure detection: https://github.com/rwth-imi/monipy
- HaMoJo — multimodal machine learning system for pediatric simulation training: https://github.com/Mohamed-Alhaskir/HaMoJo

## Awards

- DAAD Prize for Outstanding International Students, RWTH Aachen (2021)
